title: TCP Handshake Analyser
when_to_use: When analysing network captures for suspicious connection patterns

steps.title: Open PCAP
steps.instruction: Open a PCAP in Wireshark or read a PCAP summary
steps.tip: If analysing your own traffic, start capture before generating the connection

steps.title: Filter SYN Packets
steps.instruction: Filter for TCP connections: tcp.flags.syn==1 && tcp.flags.ack==0
steps.tip: This shows only initial SYN packets — the start of every TCP connection attempt

steps.title: Trace Handshakes
steps.instruction: For each SYN, trace the full handshake: SYN, SYN-ACK, ACK
steps.tip: Right-click a packet → Follow → TCP Stream to see the complete conversation

steps.title: Identify Half-Open Connections
steps.instruction: Note any half-open connections (SYN without SYN-ACK): possible scan
steps.tip: Many SYN packets to different ports from one IP = port scan. SYN flood = DoS attack

steps.title: Note RST Packets
steps.instruction: Note any RST packets: connection refused or reset
steps.tip: RST after SYN = port closed. RST after established connection = connection forcefully terminated

steps.title: Document Findings
steps.instruction: Document: source IP, dest IP, port, handshake status
steps.tip: Create a connection state table for your report. Include: endpoints, ports, state, assessment
